author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Ode to Friendship
content:
AN ODE TO FRIENDSHIP

by Dr Samuel Johnson
Friendship! Peculiar boon of heaven,
The noble mind's delight and pride,
To Men and Angels only given,
To all the lower world deny'd.
While love, a stranger to the blest,
Parent of thousand wild desires,
The human and the savage breast
Inflames alike with raging fires.
With bright, but oft destructive gleam,
Alike o'er all his lightnings fly;
Thy lambent glories only beam
Around the fav'rites of the sky.
Directress of the brave and just,
O guide me through life's darksome way,
And let the tortures of mistrust
On selfish bosoms only prey.
Thy gentle flows of guiltless joys
On fools and villains ne'er descend;
In vain for thee the monarch sighs
And hugs a flatterer for a friend.
When Virtues kindred Virtues meet,
And sister souls together join,
Thy pleasures, permanent as great,
Are all transporting, all divine.
Oh, must their ardours cease to glow
When souls to blissful climes remove?
What rais'd our Virtues here below,
Shall aid our Happiness above.

Seminar on Doctor Samuel Johnson’s "An Ode to Friendship"

Contents


1 General background to Johnson
2-8 Stanza 1.
2-4 Definition of friendship and Love in friendship
4 "Peculiar boon of heaven"
5 "The Noble mind’s delight and pride"
5-8 "To Men and Angels only given" - The nature of friendship
8-17 Stanza 2: "While love a stranger to the blest..."
10 Contrast with modern values
11-15 Sexuality within relationships and friendship
15 Intensity of feeling
17-18 Stanza 3: "With bright, but or destructive gleam..."
18 Background to Johnson
19-37 Stanza 4: "Directress of the brave..."
19 Feminine personification of Friendship
22-27 Developing Friendship
24-27 Intimacy
27 Trust, jealousy, exclusivity
29-37 Justice and legitimate use of power.
37-45 Stanza 5: "Thy gentle flows of guiltless joys..."
38 Fools - Johnson’s definition, also of Friendship
39 "Guiltless joys"
40-43 Individuality, intelligence and memory
43 "Hugs a flatterer for a friend"
45-48 Stanza 6: When "Virtues kindred Virtues meet..."
46-48 Pleasures of friendship
49 Stanza 7: "Oh must their ardours cease to glow..."
Quality and value of friendship
52-58 Limitations in developing friendship
